title: Kairos --help enrichment: quality hints in comment, create, and update help text
description:
AI coding agents frequently call --help to discover commands and capabilities before using them. This is kairos — the right moment to deliver guidance. Embedding quality hints directly in --help output means the agent receives the guidance at the exact moment it is deciding how to call the command, before it makes mistakes.

Two levels of enrichment:

1. Command-specific help (kbs comment, kbs create --help, kbs update --help): full, clear guidance on Markdown support, diagram support, and correct multiline text passing — because an agent reading these is about to use the command.

2. Top-level help (kbs --help, kbs help): a single brief line referencing the most common quality pitfall (literal backslash-n), kept very token-efficient because the agent may be scanning a broad command list.

Scenario: kbs comment --help mentions Markdown and diagram support

Given an agent calls kbs comment --help
Then the output includes a section noting that comment body supports full Markdown
And it mentions that diagrams can be embedded as fenced code blocks (mermaid, plantuml, d2)
And it shows the correct pattern for passing multiline text (heredoc or newline-containing string)
And it warns that literal backslash-n will be auto-converted with a warning

Scenario: kbs create --help mentions Markdown in description

Given an agent calls kbs create --help
Then the help for the --description option notes that Markdown is supported
And it includes a brief note about avoiding literal backslash-n sequences

Scenario: kbs update --help mentions Markdown in description

Given an agent calls kbs update --help
Then the help for the --description option notes the same quality guidance as create

Scenario: Top-level kbs --help includes a one-line quality reminder

Given an agent calls kbs --help or kbs help
Then the output includes a brief reminder such as:
  Tip: Use real newlines in --description and comments, not backslash-n. Markdown and diagrams are supported.
And this tip is clearly separate from the command listing and does not add significant noise

Scenario: Help text is token-efficient on the command-specific pages

Given an agent reads kbs comment --help
Then the quality guidance section is concise (no more than 6-8 lines)
And each sentence carries actionable information
And there is no padding or filler text
creator: ryan.porter
